# AI Copilot Instructions for AI-Toolsx-ABM Repository

## Project Overview

This repository maintains **Learning Sites.md** — a comprehensive, curated directory of free and paid learning platforms across technology, business, design, AI, and professional development. It is maintained by Abdul Basit Memon (Founder — Prologware Solutions) as part of the Resources Heaven community initiative.

Additionally, **AI-Toolsx-ABM** is a reference list of AI-powered coding tools, agents, and IDEs categorized by use case (code review, agents, UI/UX design, etc.).

## Core Patterns & Structure

### Content Organization
- **Learning Sites.md**: Markdown table format with columns: Website Name, URL, Access (Free/Paid), Category
  - Use consistent capitalization for category names (e.g., "MOOC / University Courses", "AI / Machine Learning")
  - Include creator/project attribution section at the top with portfolio, GitHub, LinkedIn, and blog links
  - Maintain "How to contribute" guidance and license/credits section at bottom
  
- **AI-Toolsx-ABM**: Plain text hierarchical list grouped by tool category (Code Review, AI Agents, Browser Agents, MCPs, UI/UX Designers, Planning Tools)
  - Entries include tool name and optional description in parentheses
  - Categories marked with `##` headers

### Data Quality Standards
- All URLs should be verified before inclusion
- Website entries require: name, working URL, access type (Free/Paid/Free & Paid), and functional category
- Tool entries should indicate if open-source, cloud-based, or specialized use case
- Cross-reference related tools within categories where applicable

### Contribution Workflow
- All additions/updates should reference the GitHub repo at: https://github.com/abm1119
- Suggest new resources via PR on the respective repo (look for `resources` or `Techdio` repos)
- For CSV/JSON exports or responsive page versions, create issues or contact via creator's portfolio

## Creator & Attribution

All instructions and content updates should preserve:
- Abdul Basit Memon as primary author/creator
- Prologware Solutions as organizational context
- Links to: Portfolio (engrabm.com), GitHub (abm1119), LinkedIn (Pakistan profile)
- License notice: Use freely for educational purposes; credit the author when republishing

## AI Agent Guidance

When adding/editing content:
1. **Verify URLs**: Test that links work and content matches description
2. **Maintain formatting consistency**: Follow existing table structure (Learning Sites.md) or list hierarchy (AI-Toolsx-ABM)
3. **Category accuracy**: Place tools/sites in most specific relevant category; avoid duplication
4. **Descriptive brevity**: If adding descriptions, keep to 2-5 words clarifying the tool's unique purpose
5. **Date awareness**: Note when tools launched or changed status (free → paid) if relevant for context

No complex build/test workflows exist. Updates are content-driven and PR-based.
